headline: Set up your public key during onboarding
subtext: The encryption step lets recipients search Proton Mail or paste a public PGP key manually so sources can send messages that only the intended recipient can read.
image_alt_text: Social graphic using a Hush Line desktop screenshot from the onboarding flow. The card shows step 2, "Now, let's set up encryption," with options to search Proton Mail for a key or paste a public PGP key manually, plus Continue and Skip setup for now buttons.

social.bluesky:
If you're setting up a tip line, add your public key during setup.

Hush Line lets recipients search Proton Mail for a key or paste a public PGP key manually, so sources can send encrypted first contact from the start.

Sign up at https://hushline.app.
social.linkedin:
If you are setting up a tip line for a newsroom, law office, school, or organizing team, encryption needs to be in place before sources rely on it.

Hush Line's onboarding flow lets recipients search for a Proton Mail key or paste a public PGP key manually, so anonymous first contact can be encrypted for the right person from the start.

Sign up at https://hushline.app.
social.mastodon:
If you're setting up a Hush Line account for a newsroom, law office, school, or organizing team, encryption should be part of setup, not an afterthought.

The onboarding flow lets recipients search Proton Mail for a key or paste a public PGP key manually so sources can send encrypted first contact from the start.

Sign up at https://hushline.app.

rationale: The shortlist was constrained by recent archive overlap. The vision candidate repeats the same screen and topic family used on 2026-04-07, and the settings-encryption desktop candidate is a closer repeat of the settings-encryption post from 2026-04-02. This onboarding encryption desktop screen still sits in the encryption family, but it gives the most distinct remaining angle by focusing on the setup workflow itself: Proton key lookup or manual PGP paste during onboarding for recipient accounts.
copy_brief: Write for recipients and staff using Hush Line day to day, not for platform admins.

matched_pull_requests.title: Support multi-recipient notifications and recipient-specific encryption
#1875 Update public submission and client-side encryption for multiple recipient keys
#1874 Support multi-recipient OpenPGP encryption and per-recipient notification delivery

title: Onboarding - Step 2 Encryption (newman)
